theater_description: Urban Grozny during the heaviest bombardment, encirclement, New Year's assault, and clearing operations. Civilian exposure is restricted to persons physically present inside city limits; military exposure includes forces in the city and immediately adjoining assault/cordon positions directly assigned to the battle.
geometry_method_note: Civilian polygon should track Grozny city proper. Military exposure footprint should include the urban area plus the immediate cordon/approach belt used to isolate and storm the city; it should not include all Russian forces in Chechnya.
military_area_note: Count army/MVD/other federal units directly assigned to the assault and blockade of Grozny, plus Chechen defenders operating inside the city and immediate urban edge.
civilian_area_note: Count only civilians remaining in Grozny, with denominator reduced over time as civilians fled or were displaced.
split_reason: City-scale siege/assault with large trapped-civilian population, dense artillery/air use, and distinctive urban combat structure. Whole-war denominators would badly misstate exposure.
quality_note: Civilian deaths and displacement are triangulated from multiple NGO/scholarly sources; combatant deaths and direct-assignment force exposure remain partly modeled.

theater_description: One-day airstrike episode centered on the town of Shali, including the market and hospital areas reportedly hit on 3 January 1995.
geometry_method_note: Town footprint is straightforward for civilian exposure, but combatant assignment and the size of the population present in the strike zone are not yet reconstructed.
military_area_note: Would require identification of Russian air assets directly tasked to the strike and any organized armed presence in or immediately around Shali.
civilian_area_note: Would require town population present on strike day, ideally clipped to market/hospital attendance patterns.
split_reason: Discrete strike episode with a clear town footprint and a strong civilian-fatality source, but insufficient denominator work completed here.
quality_note: Numerator anchor is useful, but combatant exposure and same-day civilian presence are not yet assembled.

theater_description: Village-scale shelling, assault, cordon, and house-to-house 'mopping-up' action in and around Samashki. Civilian exposure is limited to residents/IDPs remaining in the village; military exposure is limited to assault and cordon forces directly assigned to the operation plus local armed defenders.
geometry_method_note: Civilian polygon should use the village built-up area. Military polygon should use the village plus the immediately surrounding cordon/fire positions directly supporting the operation through midday 10 April.
military_area_note: Count only troops and fighters directly assigned to the Samashki operation, not all forces in western Chechnya.
civilian_area_note: Count only persons who remained in Samashki in early April 1995; do not use district or republic-wide populations.
split_reason: Village-scale MVD-led operation with strong source coverage on residents remaining, civilian deaths, and subgroup metrics; very different geometry and violence mode from Grozny.
quality_note: Civilian numerator and civilian denominator are strong; combatant status labeling and operation force size are highly disputed.

episode_name: Grozny August 1996 counteroffensive and siege reversal
theater_description: The August 1996 Chechen offensive into Grozny, siege reversal, failed Russian relief attempts, civilian panic flight, and the lead-up to the Khasavyurt ceasefire.
geometry_method_note: City footprint is clear, but the available sources collected here do not yet provide a clean civilian death/status series matched to a stable population-present trajectory.
military_area_note: Force presence includes the city garrison and attacking Chechen units, but attrition and reinforcement counts need fuller reconstruction.
civilian_area_note: Available snippets conflict on how many civilians remained versus fled during the ultimatum and bombardment window.
split_reason: Distinct belligerent structure and urban battle after a year of intermittent war; should not be collapsed into the 1994-95 Grozny episode.
quality_note: Promising episode anchor, but civilian mortality/status and denominator trajectories need dedicated reconstruction.
